summary: The article primarily profiles Michael Ilitch and the growth of Little Caesars Pizza, but highlights co-founder Marian Ilitch's crucial role in the company's financial management. It notes her insistence on unit-level profitability and her preference for manual bookkeeping to maintain a close pulse on the business.

principles.statement: Each franchise outlet must carry its own weight.
principles.rationale: To ensure the financial health and stability of the overall company by maintaining strict unit-level accountability.

principles.statement: Franchisees should do their books by hand.
principles.rationale: It provides a better, more tactile understanding of how all segments of the business are performing.

heuristics: Do the books by hand to get a better feel for the business.

signature_quotes.text: We were just married and he was my hero, my Caesar. But he hadn’t accomplished anything yet, so he was my little Caesar.
signature_quotes.context: Explaining how she came up with the name for the company.
